source_old_req: The ACCELERO thread shall execute periodically with a period of 80 ms and a priority of 5, and its compute execution time shall be within 0 ms to 4 ms. The thread shall process incoming accelerometer data from the acc_in_data_x, acc_in_data_y, and acc_in_data_z input ports, and produce corresponding output values on the acc_out_data_x, acc_out_data_y, and acc_out_data_z output ports. The thread shall ensure that the values written to acc_out_data_x, acc_out_data_y, and acc_out_data_z are determined solely by the current values received on acc_in_data_x, acc_in_data_y, and acc_in_data_z, along with internal computations performed during the thread's execution. The thread shall maintain data type integrity for all port connections, ensuring that the data types of acc_out_data_x, acc_out_data_y, and acc_out_data_z match the declared AHRS::Data_Types::FLOAT32_TYPE_AADL type. Environmental assumptions shall be established regarding the validity and timing constraints of the input data, specifically that acc_in_data_x, acc_in_data_y, and acc_in_data_z are valid FLOAT32 values received within a latency range of 10 ms to 20 ms.
natural_requirement: The ACCELERO component shall define the implementation-level signal routing for ACCELERO.IMPL. In the implementation layer, ACCELERO.IMPL shall route data flow from accelero_sub.acc_out_data_x to acc_out_data_x through ACC_PC1 and data flow from accelero_sub.acc_out_data_y to acc_out_data_y through ACC_PC2.

requirement_items.text: The ACCELERO.IMPL implementation shall route the data flow from accelero_sub.acc_out_data_x to acc_out_data_x through connection ACC_PC1.
requirement_items.source_text_before_behavior_style_rewrit: The ACCELERO.IMPL implementation shall declare connection ACC_PC1 from accelero_sub.acc_out_data_x to acc_out_data_x.

requirement_items.text: The ACCELERO.IMPL implementation shall route the data flow from accelero_sub.acc_out_data_y to acc_out_data_y through connection ACC_PC2.
requirement_items.source_text_before_behavior_style_rewrit: The ACCELERO.IMPL implementation shall declare connection ACC_PC2 from accelero_sub.acc_out_data_y to acc_out_data_y.

quality_policy.traceable: Every item has trace_refs and expected_check metadata tied to AADL facts.
quality_policy.principle: Every generated sentence must name an explicit property assignment or structural connection; ordinary feature declarations are not used as rebuilt requirements.

natural_requirement_before_behavior_styl: The ACCELERO.IMPL implementation shall declare connection ACC_PC1 from accelero_sub.acc_out_data_x to acc_out_data_x. The ACCELERO.IMPL implementation shall declare connection ACC_PC2 from accelero_sub.acc_out_data_y to acc_out_data_y.

behavior_style_policy.basis: front-110 AGREE-derived requirements favor implementable behavior, constants, assumptions, guarantees, routing, and bounded properties.
behavior_style_policy.rule: Do not describe unsupported validity/legal-boundary semantics unless the AADL model exposes variables, values, or properties that can implement them.

natural_requirement_before_paragraph_rew: The ACCELERO.IMPL implementation shall route the data flow from accelero_sub.acc_out_data_x to acc_out_data_x through connection ACC_PC1. The ACCELERO.IMPL implementation shall route the data flow from accelero_sub.acc_out_data_y to acc_out_data_y through connection ACC_PC2.

paragraph_style_policy.rule: Req.txt is written as a natural-language requirement paragraph; per-item traceability stays in requirement_items.
paragraph_style_policy.basis: Front-110 AGREE-derived requirements generally combine function, assumptions, guarantees, and implementation notes into 1-4 sentence paragraphs.
